Patch Set 2:

(3 comments)

I'm torn between +1 on this (since it appears to be fixing a real issue) and -1 (because it may be fixing the bug in the wrong place).

Could you please update LU-18895 with more details about the error case (e.g. logs, or at least a better description of where -EINVAL comes from) so that the scope of the problem can be better understood.

Trance the root cause to https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56341 which was later fixed by https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57920

So while this would work around an unfixed server, the bug is not in a released lustre version.

commit.subject: LU-18895 llite: unlink should not fail with -EINVAL
commit.message:
LU-18895 llite: unlink should not fail with -EINVAL

During FOFB testing with mdtest files failed to unlink
with -EINVAL.

__x64_sys_unlink+0x23/0x30
 do_unlinkat+0x292/0x2f0
  vfs_unlink+0xf6/0x1d0
   ll_unlink+0x38b/0x7b0 [lustre]
    lmv_unlink+0x25c/0x760 [lmv]
     mdc_unlink+0x218/0x680 [mdc]
      mdc_reint+0x22/0xd0 [mdc]
       ptlrpc_queue_wait+0x86/0x240 [ptlrpc]
       ...
	rc = set->set_rc;
	list_for_each_entry(req, &set->set_requests, rq_set_chain) {
		LASSERT(req->rq_phase == RQ_PHASE_COMPLETE);
		if (req->rq_status != 0)
			rc = req->rq_status; <----- -EINVAL
	}
       ...

vfs_unlink will trigger d_revalidate if unlink() handler
returns -ESTALE and retry the unlink() handler.

ll_unlink() should to return -ESTALE to trigger d_revalidate
to clear cases where the dentry may be stale rather than fail
immediately.

HPE-bug-id: LUS-12736
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I8d71d74b5e49095e721ca7b7122c7f01f5eb7a10
